steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends by stating that understanding how to prevent horizontal gene transfer 'might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens.' The question asks what the passage most strongly suggests — so the correct choice must reflect this emphasis on potential real-world application.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: introduces a debate about whether horizontal gene transfer is beneficial or harmful — a framing the passage never presents. ✗
  • Choice B: claims horizontal gene transfer is 'more common' than some alternative — no such comparison appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C: suggests a hypothetical controversy about research priorities the passage doesn't address. ✗
  • Choice D — studying horizontal gene transfer could yield beneficial applications: directly reflects the passage's conclusion about mitigating dangerous pathogens. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
